title: allium carinatum subsp. pulchellum
intro: Allium carinatum subsp. pulchellum, also known as the keel-flowered tumble mustard or keel-edged ornamental onion, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the onion genus (Allium) that is native to Europe. This plant is valued for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may be attributed to the presence of sulfur compounds and other bioactive molecules. The health benefits of Allium carinatum subsp. pulchellum include its possible use in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as colds and bronchitis, as well as its potential to support cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
